reforms.description: The government plans to expand targeted grant programmes that support firms in lagging sectors or technology adoption, aiming to enhance public R&D funding and improve the overall innovation landscape.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing public R&D funding is expected to stimulate innovation and productivity in lagging sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform is currently announced and not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The expansion of targeted grant programmes represents a significant but narrower reform in the innovation funding landscape.
reforms.source_quote: The government should continue expanding targeted grant programmes that support firms in lagging sectors or technology adoption.

reforms.description: The government is strengthening its programmes to boost the number of students in tech-related studies to counteract increasing sectoral shortages, including initiatives like 'Youth for Local Government' to integrate high-quality human capital into disadvantaged local authorities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing the number of students in tech-related fields is expected to address sectoral shortages and enhance the skilled workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been implemented to strengthen human capital in tech-related studies.
reforms.importance_rationale: Strengthening human capital initiatives is a significant reform aimed at addressing skill shortages in the tech sector.
reforms.source_quote: Several development instruments for local authorities have been set up. For example, 'Youth for Local Government' is integrating high-quality human capital into disadvantaged local authorities.

reforms.description: The government approved a comprehensive package worth NIS 100 billion to increase health capacity and support small- and medium-sized businesses, which includes significant infrastructure investment to facilitate recovery from the COVID-19 crisis.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The investment in SMEs and infrastructure is expected to stimulate economic recovery and innovation in the startup ecosystem.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been implemented as part of the government's response to the COVID-19 crisis.
reforms.importance_rationale: The NIS 100 billion package represents a major structural reform aimed at economic recovery and support for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: On 8 April parliament approved an initial package worth NIS 80 billion (5.6% of GDP) to increase the health capacity and shield households and firms from the impact of the crisis.

reforms.description: A new insolvency law was implemented to promote financial rehabilitation for debtors, maximize returns to creditors, and streamline procedures, thereby reducing bureaucratic burdens.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining insolvency procedures can enhance the business environment and support economic recovery.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been implemented to improve insolvency procedures.
reforms.importance_rationale: The implementation of a new insolvency law represents a major reform in the financial rehabilitation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: In September 2019, a new insolvency law came into force, aiming to promote the debtor’s financial rehabilitation; maximise the return to creditors; increase the certainty and stability of the law, and shorten procedures and reduce the bureaucratic burden.

reforms.description: The Law for the Encouragement of Capital Investments (LECI) was amended to further encourage intellectual property-based activity, introducing a reduced tax rate on intellectual property income for preferred technology enterprises.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging intellectual property-based activity is expected to stimulate innovation and investment in technology enterprises.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been implemented to encourage capital investments in technology.
reforms.importance_rationale: The amendment to the LECI represents a significant reform in the governance of capital investments.
reforms.source_quote: In 2017 the LECI was amended to further encourage intellectual property-based activity, and a so-called innovation or IP box regime for (special) preferred technology enterprises was introduced.

reforms.description: The Israeli government established the 'Increasing Productivity in Industry' and 'Implementing Advanced Manufacturing Technologies' programmes to support productivity-enhancing investments in traditional manufacturing sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Supporting productivity-enhancing investments is expected to improve efficiency and innovation in traditional manufacturing sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The programmes have been implemented to support productivity in industry.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of these programmes represents a significant reform aimed at enhancing productivity.
reforms.source_quote: To promote technology adoption by lagging sectors, the authorities have more recently established several grant programmes for firms in traditional manufacturing industries.

reforms.description: Israel is considering the introduction of expenditure-based R&D tax incentives to complement direct R&D funding, which could help small and young firms overcome financial constraints in conducting research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Introducing R&D tax incentives could enhance the financial viability of research for small and young firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform is currently under consideration and has not yet been implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: Considering R&D tax incentives represents a significant reform in the innovation support landscape.
reforms.source_quote: Expenditure-based R&D tax incentives such as tax credits could avoid some of the drawbacks of income-based incentives and complement direct R&D funding.
